nodes.label: Browser / Playground
nodes.role: The playground UI and the embedded AgentChatSlice. Sends /invoke or /messages, renders the streamed reply, and owns conversation state client-side.
owns: Building the WorkflowInvokeRequest / /messages request
Rendering Vercel UI Message Stream parts via useChat
Keeping full message history and resending it every turn (the runtime is cold)
Fulfilling client tools (e.g. request_connection) and returning results next turn
nodes.notes: The browser is the only place client tools actually execute; the runner never runs them.

nodes.label: Agenta Gateway / Workflow Route
nodes.role: The generic Agenta workflow HTTP surface. Negotiates batch vs. stream from the Accept header and dispatches to the agent workflow handler; shared by every workflow type, not agent-specific.
owns: WorkflowInvokeRequest/WorkflowBatchResponse envelope
Accept-header negotiation (JSON vs text/event-stream)
Registering /messages only for agent routes (flags={"is_agent": true})
nodes.notes: A change here ripples to every workflow type, not just agents.

nodes.label: Agent Service (Python)
nodes.role: Owns what to run: parses AgentConfig, resolves tools/MCP/secrets, builds SessionConfig, selects a Backend and Harness, records usage on the workflow span.
owns: AgentConfig.from_params parsing (harness, sandbox, agents_md, tools, mcp_servers, runner.permissions.default)
select_backend (always SandboxAgentBackend in the deployed stack)
Building SessionConfig and running one cold turn via a Harness over a Backend
Recording usage on the live workflow span
nodes.notes: Runs inside the shared 'services' container as a normal Agenta workflow, not a separate process.

nodes.label: Vercel Protocol Adapter
nodes.role: Converts the browser chat contract to and from the neutral runtime: folds Vercel UIMessage input into neutral Message objects, mints/validates session_id, and projects live AgentEvent objects into Vercel UI Message Stream parts framed as SSE. Used only by /messages.
owns: session_id minting (sess_ prefix) and validation (^[A-Za-z0-9._:-]{1,128}$)
UIMessage -> neutral ChatMessage[] conversion, incl. tool_call/tool_result and {approved} envelopes
AgentEvent -> Vercel stream-part mapping (text, reasoning, tool-input/-output, approval, data, file, usage, error, done)
SSE framing and the terminal data: [DONE]
nodes.notes: /invoke never touches this adapter; it reads data.inputs.messages directly.

nodes.label: Tool & Secret Resolution (SDK)
nodes.role: Turns declared tool/MCP config into resolved, per-run specs: builtin names pass through, gateway/reference/platform tools resolve to CallbackToolSpec, code tools get named secrets injected, MCP servers get secrets when the feature flag is on.
owns: resolve_tools per type (builtin/gateway/code/client/reference/platform)
resolve_mcp_servers, gated by AGENTA_AGENT_ENABLE_MCP
resolve_connection: least-privilege single vault-key selection
nodes.notes: The SDK owns the generic algorithm; Agenta-specific HTTP calls are injected as platform adapters. The SDK never imports the service.

nodes.label: AgentaHarness (pi_agenta)
nodes.role: Maps neutral SessionConfig into Pi config plus Agenta's forced opinion: forced tools, forced skills (getting-started), a base AGENTS.md preamble, and a persona string.
owns: Force-unioning the getting-started skill onto every pi_agenta run
Injecting a hardcoded append-system persona preamble
nodes.notes: Runs on the same sandbox-agent/pi ACP path as pi_core; the runner treats it as plain 'pi' plus layered extras. Content (preamble/persona/skills) is still placeholder.

nodes.label: LocalBackend (SDK stub)
nodes.role: A public Backend adapter shape intended to run a harness on the local machine without the sandbox-agent daemon. Not implemented.
nodes.notes: create_sandbox and create_session raise NotImplementedError. select_backend never chooses it in the deployed stack; SandboxAgentBackend is used for every run regardless of harness.

nodes.label: Agenta API: Tools & Secrets Endpoints
nodes.role: The API-side endpoints that validate connections, enrich gateway tool metadata, execute tool calls server-side, resolve named secrets, and run reference-workflow or platform-op tool calls.
owns: POST /tools/resolve: connection validation + Composio catalog enrichment -> call_ref slug
POST /tools/call: routes by call_ref prefix (tools.* Composio, workflow.* reference, tools.agenta.* reserved handler)
POST /secrets/resolve: named-secret lookup for code tools and MCP servers
POST /tools/discover: Composio semantic search translated to Agenta capabilities
nodes.notes: The Composio key and connection auth never leave this component.

nodes.label: Composio
nodes.role: The external gateway tool provider. Agenta's API executes the resolved action against Composio's catalog on the connected account.
owns: Running the actual third-party integration action (e.g. github create_issue)
nodes.notes: Only the composio provider is supported today; other gateway providers raise.

nodes.label: Agent Runner Sidecar
nodes.role: The Node package that runs the actual agent turn. Serves one /run contract over two entrypoints (HTTP server on :8765, or a CLI subprocess), and drives one engine: a harness over ACP through the sandbox-agent daemon.
owns: AgentRunRequest/AgentRunResult wire contract (protocol.ts)
run-plan construction, harness selection remap (pi_core/pi_agenta -> pi, claude -> claude)
Capability probing and the fail-loud tool-delivery assertion
Tool dispatch (callback/code/client), the file-based relay, and the ACP approval responder
OTel span construction for non-self-instrumenting harnesses (Claude); usage writeback readback for Pi
nodes.notes: Standalone pnpm package, not part of the web workspace. Naming drift: current docs and code say 'runner' (services/runner/); older docs say 'sandbox-agent' (services/agent/); it is the SAME component. One HTTP server process, no worker pool; each /run spawns its own process tree.

nodes.label: sandbox-agent daemon
nodes.role: The daemon process the runner starts per turn. Resolves a harness id to an ACP adapter, starts the harness CLI (local or inside a Daytona sandbox), and speaks ACP back to the runner.
owns: Resolving 'pi' -> pi-acp adapter -> pi CLI; 'claude' -> claude-agent-acp adapter -> claude CLI
Local vs. Daytona sandbox provisioning
nodes.notes: One sandbox-agent process per /run; torn down in the finally path once the turn ends.

nodes.label: ACP adapter: pi-acp
nodes.role: The ACP adapter that speaks to the Pi CLI. Does not bundle Pi; spawns the pi binary from PATH (pinned via PI_ACP_PI_COMMAND).
owns: Starting the pi CLI as a subprocess
Translating ACP calls to/from Pi's own protocol
nodes.notes: pi-acp forwards no MCP servers to Pi; Pi 0.79.4 does not support MCP.

nodes.label: ACP adapter: claude-agent-acp
nodes.role: The ACP adapter that speaks to the Claude Code CLI. Downloads the claude CLI over HTTPS on first use rather than bundling it.
owns: Starting the claude CLI
Relaying session/update notifications and permission requests over ACP
nodes.notes: Requires ca-certificates in the sidecar image for the HTTPS download to verify.

nodes.label: Harness CLI: Pi
nodes.role: The Pi coding-agent CLI. Runs the model loop, calls tools natively via the bundled extension, and self-instruments its own OTel spans.
owns: The model loop and native tool execution
Building its own system prompt from AGENTS.md + SYSTEM/APPEND_SYSTEM overrides
nodes.notes: Default harness. Per-request model override is not honored on this path; pi-acp accepts only its default model.

nodes.label: Harness CLI: Claude Code
nodes.role: The Claude Code CLI. Takes tools over MCP, gates tool use behind its own permission prompt (checked first against a rendered settings.json, then the ACP responder), and does not self-instrument.
owns: The model loop and MCP-delivered tool calls
Its own permission prompt / settings.json gate
nodes.codePath: external: Claude Code CLI (proprietary, installed at runtime from Anthropic, never baked into the image)
nodes.notes: Traced entirely from the runner's ACP event-stream reconstruction, since it brings no Agenta extension.

nodes.label: Pi Extension (agenta.ts)
nodes.role: A single esbuild-bundled extension installed into Pi's agent directory on every run. Delivers Agenta's resolved tools the Pi-native way and self-instruments tracing, both driven entirely by env vars.
owns: pi.registerTool for each resolved public tool spec (from AGENTA_TOOL_PUBLIC_SPECS)
The tool_call hook that reports every Pi builtin call over the relay as a permission record
before_agent_start grant-list enforcement for builtin tool names
Self-instrumented OTel spans (invoke_agent > turn N > chat/execute_tool)
Usage writeback to AGENTA_USAGE_OUT on agent_end
nodes.notes: Inert (no-op) when none of its env vars are set, so it is safe to install globally.

nodes.label: agenta-tools MCP Bridge
nodes.role: A small MCP server the runner serves on a loopback HTTP endpoint, so non-Pi harnesses (Claude) can take resolved tools over MCP. It is a tool DELIVERY vehicle, not a user-declared MCP server.
owns: Exposing public tool metadata (names, descriptions, schemas) only
Relaying each call back to the runner over the file relay for execution
Pausing client tools inside its tools/call handler for the cross-turn HITL flow
nodes.notes: Local-only: unreachable on a remote (Daytona) sandbox, so a non-Pi run carrying any custom tool is refused up front there rather than silently dropped.

nodes.label: Tool Relay (file-based)
nodes.role: Executes tools and permission checks the runner (not the harness) is responsible for: gateway/code/reference/platform callback tools, and now Pi's own builtins, relayed through files rather than a direct call when the child process cannot reach the runner in-memory.
owns: Polling the relay dir for *.req.json, executing against the private spec, writing *.res.json
Enforcing decide() for Pi (gateway, code, and builtins) since Pi has no harness-side settings gate
The permission-record protocol (kind:"permission") shared with execute requests
nodes.notes: The same loop backs both a local filesystem relay and a Daytona sandbox filesystem relay. Poll cadence starts at 300ms and backs off to 1500ms on an idle turn.

nodes.label: Permission Decision Module
nodes.role: The one shared decision function both gates (the ACP responder and the tool relay) call, so a tool's effective permission can never disagree between the two enforcement points.
owns: permissionsFromRequest: parsing the wire permissions block, applying the kill switch
effectivePermission: explicit tool permission -> MCP server permission -> rule match -> policy default
decide: deny/allow short-circuit, else consult stored decisions, else pendingApproval
nodes.notes: A pure decision function with no I/O; both the responder and the relay call it directly.

nodes.label: ACP Approval Responder
nodes.role: Answers permission gates that a harness raises itself over ACP (Claude Code today, via session.onPermissionRequest) and client-tool requests across the pause/resume boundary.
owns: ConversationDecisions: recovering approvals/denials and client-tool outputs from replayed message history
approvedCallKey: the stable name+canonicalized-args resume anchor
decisionToReply: mapping allow/deny onto the harness's own ACP reply vocabulary ("once"/"reject")
nodes.notes: Gate 2 for Claude: its own .claude/settings.json is Gate 1 and decides most calls before this responder is ever consulted.

nodes.label: Session Persistence (cold, in-memory)
nodes.role: An in-process session persistence driver the sandbox-agent engine keeps for the duration of one /run process only.
nodes.notes: Does not survive across turns, so it does not make the runtime warm. There is no durable session store, no history-load endpoint, and no ACP session/load resume wired anywhere.

nodes.label: Daytona Sandbox VM
nodes.role: A remote Daytona VM the runner creates per turn when sandbox=daytona. The harness login, Pi extension, AGENTS.md, skills, and system-prompt files are pushed into it over the Daytona filesystem API.
owns: Running the harness CLI remotely, isolated from the runner host
The remote side of the file relay (sandbox.runProcess for ls, readFsFile/writeFsFile for the relay files)
nodes.notes: One sandbox per turn, torn down after (ephemeral, paired with a non-zero SANDBOX_AGENT_DAYTONA_AUTOSTOP_MINUTES, default 15, as a leak backstop). Agenta ships a build recipe, not a built snapshot; the operator runs it in their own Daytona account. Cold VM start dominates end-to-end latency on this path.

nodes.label: Tracing / OTel Pipeline
nodes.role: Threads W3C trace context from the caller's /invoke span into the run and nests agent spans under it: invoke_agent (AGENT) > turn N (CHAIN) > chat (LLM) + execute_tool (TOOL). Rolls run usage back onto the workflow span.
owns: trace_context() / run_context() in tracing.py: filling context.propagation and telemetry from the live workflow span
Pi: self-instrumented spans via the bundled extension, real per-call token counts
Claude (and any non-self-instrumenting harness): spans built by the runner from the ACP event stream
Usage rollup onto the workflow span from the /run result
nodes.notes: Pi's spans and the workflow span ship to Agenta in separate OTel batches; usage writeback (AGENTA_USAGE_OUT) is the extra hop that lets the workflow span show Pi's real tokens anyway.

nodes.label: Triggers
nodes.role: Intended event-to-agent mapping: a provider port, a Compose.io adapter, Agenta-owned trigger state, so an agent can be invoked by an external event rather than a chat turn.
nodes.notes: Trigger discovery (discover_triggers) exists as a read-only platform tool op, but trigger lifecycle, subscription, and event-to-agent invocation are not implemented in the agent workflow code.
